added_sources.title: An Improved Algorithm for Computing Logarithms over GF(p) and Its Cryptographic Significance
added_sources.authors: Stephen C. Pohlig; Martin E. Hellman
added_sources.venue: IEEE Transactions on Information Theory, 24(1), pp. 106-110

relations.basis: Provides the foundational smooth-order decomposition rationale behind selecting a large prime-order cryptographic subgroup.

verified_against: Stanford-hosted scan of the IEEE Transactions on Information Theory correspondence

added_sources.review_boundary: The paper is formulated for finite-field discrete logarithms. Its value here is the general subgroup-order decomposition baseline. It is not a secp256k1-specific attack; secp256k1's prime subgroup order removes a smooth-factor shortcut but does not prove hardness against other mechanisms.

reported_results.condition: p-1 has only small prime factors in the paper's finite-field setting
finite-field exponentiation used as a one-way function
reported_results.value: p-1 must have a large prime factor

added_sources.title: Improved Quantum Circuits for Elliptic Curve Discrete Logarithms
added_sources.authors: Thomas Häner; Samuel Jaques; Michael Naehrig; Martin Roetteler; Mathias Soeken

relations.basis: Improves the earlier logical-circuit resource estimates through windowing, pebbling, modular-inversion changes, and explicit cost tradeoffs.

added_sources.review_boundary: Logical-circuit estimates only. The paper explicitly does not choose a physical architecture or establish deployable fault-tolerant hardware.

reported_results.condition: 256-bit elliptic curve, width-oriented comparison with Roetteler et al.
relative to Roetteler et al. estimate
relative to Roetteler et al. estimate

added_sources.title: Performance Analysis of a Repetition Cat Code Architecture: Computing 256-bit Elliptic Curve Logarithm in 9 Hours with 126133 Cat Qubits
added_sources.authors: Élie Gouzien; Diego Ruiz; Francois-Marie Le Régent; Jérémie Guillaud; Nicolas Sangouard

relations.basis: Maps logical ECDLP circuitry into a concrete repetition-cat-code architecture with explicit error and timing assumptions.

added_sources.review_boundary: Architecture-specific projection. It assumes a single- to two-photon loss ratio of 1e-5, a 500 ns cycle, and cat states averaging 19 photons. The authors themselves flag the 19-photon regime as experimentally challenging. This is not evidence of present hardware feasibility.

reported_results.condition: secp256k1, repetition cat code architecture under stated assumptions

added_sources.title: How to Compute a 256-bit Elliptic Curve Private Key with Only 50 Million Toffoli Gates

relations.basis: Adds architecture-independent batching/state-reuse changes and evaluates active-volume versus strict 2D surface-code architectures.

added_sources.review_boundary: The headline is an architecture and amortization study, not a current-hardware claim. Throughput per key relies on non-local active-volume assumptions and on reusing stored state or spreading modular-division cost across parallel instances; it must not be read as isolated single-key latency without those conditions.

reported_results.condition: active-volume architecture versus strict 2D-local baseline, depending on operating regime
10% threshold, 10 us code cycle, non-local connections

relations.basis: Foundational rationale for avoiding smooth group orders; applied here only as subgroup-order context.
Improves the logical circuit and cost tradeoffs for 256-bit prime-field ECDLP.
Projects ECDLP logical circuitry into a repetition-cat-code physical architecture.
Adds active-volume architecture and amortized algorithmic optimizations.

reviewed_scope: the missing algorithmic quantum baseline between Roetteler 2017 and the 2023-2026 resource estimates
physical architecture projections for 256-bit ECDLP under cat-code and active-volume assumptions
the smooth-order subgroup decomposition baseline needed to interpret secp256k1's prime subgroup order
decision_impact: No new classical proposal selected. The additions close omitted baseline and architecture-comparison gaps; they do not authorize a new experiment.
limitations: Architecture estimates depend strongly on error models, connectivity, code cycles, state reuse, batching, and other hardware assumptions.
Pohlig-Hellman is a finite-field DLP paper; its secp256k1 role here is general subgroup-order context rather than a direct curve attack.
All four PDFs are hash-pinned locally but do not yet have Drive PDF file IDs.
Exact page-level atomic claim extraction remains queued.
